thinking.text:
I found the relevant document about referral statuses. According to the "Internal: Understanding Credit Card Referral Statuses" document (ID: doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_001), the statuses are:

- REJECTED — the user has too many referral processes going on

So the Platinum Rewards Card referral was rejected because the user had too many referral processes going on. Let me think about this more carefully.

Looking at the timeline:
1. Bronze Rewards Card - COMPLETE - Oct 20, 2025
2. Gold Rewards Card - COMPLETE - Oct 22, 2025
3. Platinum Rewards Card - REJECTED - Oct 25, 2025
4. Silver Rewards Card - IN_PROGRESS - Nov 5, 2025

And from the knowledge base: "You can receive at most 2 referral bonuses in any rolling 7-day window." 

The Bronze (Oct 20) and Gold (Oct 22) were both within a 7-day window, so that's two referrals. Then the Platinum referral on Oct 25 - that's about 3-5 days after the Gold one, so that's too close to the previous two successful referrals.

Wait, but actually the REJECTED status says "the user has too many referral processes going on." Let me also consider the weekly limit from doc_credit_cards_credit_cards_(general)_002: "You can receive at most 2 referral bonuses in any rolling 7-day window."

So the Platinum Rewards Card referral was on Oct 25, 2025. The Bronze (COMPLETE) was Oct 20, and Gold (COMPLETE) was Oct 22. These two completed referrals were within the same rolling 7-day window. The Platinum referral then... hmm, it says REJECTED means "the user has too many referral processes going on."

Let me re-read more carefully. The REJECTED status description says "the user has too many referral processes going on." This could mean that at the time the Platinum referral was processed (Oct 25), there were already 2 completed referrals (Bronze on Oct 20, Gold on Oct 22) within the same rolling 7-day window, causing the system to reject the Platinum one.

Actually, looking at the weekly limit info: "You can receive at most 2 referral bonuses in any rolling 7-day window" and "If you attempt to receive more than 2 referrals in a single week, the third referral (and any subsequent referrals that week) will be automatically denied."

The Bronze (Oct 20) and Gold (Oct 22) were completed within the same 7-day window. The Platinum referral process started on Oct 25, which is still within the 7-day rolling window from Oct 20. So that would be the 3rd referral in a 7-day window, and it was automatically denied.

So the reason is that there were too many referral processes going on at the same time, specifically because there were already 2 completed referral bonuses within a rolling 7-day window.

worker_attempts.visible_answer:
Great question! I looked into our records and found the answer. According to our system, the **REJECTED** status for the Platinum Rewards Card referral on October 25, 2025, was due to there being **too many referral processes going on at that time**.

Here's why: Our referral program has a **weekly limit of 2 referral bonuses per rolling 7-day window**. Looking at your timeline:

- **Bronze Rewards Card** — Completed on **Oct 20**
- **Gold Rewards Card** — Completed on **Oct 22**
- **Platinum Rewards Card** — Processed on **Oct 25**

The Platinum Rewards Card referral fell within the same 7-day rolling window as your two earlier completed referrals. Since you already had **two successful referrals** within that window, the system automatically rejected the third one (the Platinum Rewards Card) as per our policy.

Unfortunately, auto-denied referrals due to the weekly limit **cannot be reinstated**. However, your friend is welcome to re-apply using a new referral link, as long as enough time has passed since your last two successful bonuses to fall under the cap.

Would you like me to help with anything else?
